channel.title: He Say – Crypto Trading
channel.description: Recent content in Crypto Trading on He Say

item.title: Trend Momentum Indicator (Vortex Indicator) for Detecting Market Phase Reversals
item.description:
The Vortex Trend Indicator (VI), developed by Etienne Botes and Douglas Siepman in 2010, is an effective technical analysis tool for identifying the onset and confirmation of market trends [cite: 1, 5, 13]. Inspired by natural vortex flows, the indica

Fees are the main enemy of any trading strategy. To maximize your profit from every market move, you need a platform with minimal charges. I highly recommend MEXC: Join MEXC

tor comprises two oscillating lines: +VI (positive movement) and -VI (negative movement) [cite: 2, 4, 5, 13]. It measures the strength of directional price movements, making it invaluable for spotting market phase shifts [cite: 1, 5, 9].

Calculation and Operation

The Vortex Indicator’s calculation is based on analyzing price bars over a specific period (typically 14 days) [cite: 1, 3, 6, 11]. Key components include the True Range (TR) and Vortex Movements (VM+ and VM-) [cite: 1, 10, 11, 13]. VM+ measures the difference between the current high and the previous bar’s low, while VM- measures the difference between the current low and the previous bar’s high [cite: 1, 4, 6, 13]. These values are normalized using TR, forming the +VI and -VI lines [cite: 1, 11, 13]. The greater the distance between corresponding points of the current and previous bar, the stronger the movement reflected by the indicator [cite: 6].

Signals and Interpretation

Interpreting the Vortex Indicator focuses on the relative positioning and crossovers of the +VI and -VI lines [cite: 2, 3, 8, 9, 11]. When +VI is above -VI, it signals an uptrend (a bullish market) [cite: 2, 4, 9, 11]. A dominance of -VI over +VI indicates a downtrend (a bearish market) [cite: 2, 3, 4, 9, 11]. Line crossovers serve as primary trading signals: +VI crossing -VI from bottom to top is often interpreted as a buy signal; the inverse crossover (-VI over +VI) points to a sell signal [cite: 1, 3, 5, 9, 11]. A wide divergence between the lines implies a strengthening of the current trend [cite: 3, 4, 13].

Identifying Phase Shifts

The Vortex Indicator excels at identifying market phase shifts, demonstrating transitions from consolidation to trend or from one trend to another [cite: 1, 3, 5, 8, 13]. When the Vortex lines oscillate closely together, it often signals sideways movement or market indecision [cite: 4]. A sharp crossover, accompanied by the lines diverging, indicates the emergence of a new, strong trend [cite: 3, 4].

Indicator Advantages

VI stands out for its simplicity and clear signals, empowering traders to effectively analyze trends [cite: 3, 15]. It operates across various timeframes and is suitable for diverse assets [cite: 2, 3, 6, 11, 14]. VI identifies trend inception and helps pinpoint reversal moments [cite: 3, 5, 13, 14]. It’s a versatile tool for various strategies.
